messages.content: This batch strategy reduces carbon emissions by 56,932.2 gCO₂e, with a negligible cost increase of $0.46. The majority of recommendations (1579) are in the 'eu-west-1' region, resulting in a 6.17 kg carbon reduction. The 'us-west-2' region has the largest carbon reduction at 48.29 kg, with a slight cost increase of $0.46. With 99.93% of recommendations classified as 'low' risk, this batch is a sound optimization strategy, balancing cost and carbon efficiency while respecting SLA constraints.

I reject this batch proposal due to excessive concentration risk. The proposal targets 1579 jobs in 'eu-west-1', 829 jobs in 'us-west-2', and 386 jobs in 'us-east-1', far exceeding the limit of 15 jobs per region. This poses a significant risk to system stability. Although the proposal claims a 56,932.2 gCO₂e carbon reduction and a negligible cost increase of $0.46, I must prioritize risk mitigation. I will escalate this to human review for further assessment.

issues: Concentration risk: 1579 jobs targeted at eu-west-1 (limit: 15)
Concentration risk: 829 jobs targeted at us-west-2 (limit: 15)
Concentration risk: 386 jobs targeted at us-east-1 (limit: 15)
